description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles, with a prominent cross-gabled section extending from the main structure. The exterior walls are clad in weathered gray wooden planks, accented by timber framing and a lower section of textured beige stone or stucco. Windows are few and mostly rectangular, with one side displaying a set of four-paned windows and another a small dormer window beneath the roof. The architectural style suggests a rustic or cottage-like appearance, with exposed beams and a slightly asymmetrical layout. The house appears compact, with a raised foundation and minimal decorative elements.
description: A rustic, gray wooden house with a steep gable roof, timber framing, and stone-textured lower walls. Features include cross-gabled sections, a dormer window, and minimal decorative details.
description_view_0: This view highlights the cross-gabled roof, a four-paned window, and the textured lower wall section.
description_view_1: The front gable and symmetrical roofline are visible, with no windows or doors apparent on this side.
description_view_2: Shows the side gable, timber framing, and the continuation of the stone-textured base.
description_view_3: Displays the main gable end, a small dormer window, and the raised foundation with stone texture.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway is under a small gable with a modest overhang, no porch or columns, and minimal decorative elements.
